data.title: YZi Labs has incubated 17 projects; let's see which hardcore sectors CZ and He Yi are betting on.
data.description:
Compiled by: Jinse Finance

YZi Labs officially announced the incubation projects for the second season of EASY Residency on social media. YZi Labs stated that the second season brings together startups developing in the fields of Web3, artificial intelligence, and biotechnology, reflecting YZi Labs' belief that the next decade will be shaped by the convergence of three forces:

  • Blockchain will unlock ownership, scalability, and global liquidity.

  • Artificial intelligence will accelerate the development of creativity, workforce, and intelligence.

  • Biotechnology Helps Extend Healthy Lifespans YZi Labs and EASY Residency support projects that create groundbreaking technologies and unlock global distribution at scale—from AI-driven drug discovery and robotic automation to finance and infrastructure that power a tokenized future. Here is a list of projects incubated in Season 2: 42.space: Trading real-world events such as Meme Coin.

    Project Team：

    Leo Zhang (Founder): Former Head of Research at a New York-based cryptocurrency fund; previously worked in the equity derivatives departments of KKR and Morgan Stanley; Bachelor of Science in Mathematics from Stanford University

    Steve Kim (CTO): Founding engineer at several startups (worked at companies such as Walmart/Discord); Bachelor of Science in Computer Science from Stanford University

    Aleksi Ollikainen-Read (Strategy/Operations/Legal): Former Head of Strategy and Operations at Consensys SMG; former professor of law at Oxford University; Fellow at Harvard University/Max Planck Institute; Member of the Law Society of England and Wales (2018). Investment Rationale: 42.space is an asset issuance protocol that significantly expands the scope of prediction markets. By combining curves to build a token issuance platform, 42.space allows users to create and trade assets related to real-world events. Trading is like liquid tokens; settlement is like prediction markets. This innovative mechanism was built by a strong team comprised of Stanford and Harvard alumni and Wall Street veterans, combining cutting-edge engineering and high-level financial strategies. In partnership with BNB Chain, 42.space expands the horizons of DeFi by introducing novel, event-based assets to a large user base. 4D Labs: Data Collection, Model Development, and Application of Spatial Intelligent Large Models

    Project Team：

    Bruce Bai (CEO): PhD from Zhejiang University; serial entrepreneur, previously founded two computer vision startups.

    Ian Y. Xu (Co-founder): Postdoctoral Fellow from the University of Michigan; PhD from the Chinese University of Hong Kong; former employee of Meta Reality Labs and SenseTime.

    Ian Y. Xu (Co-founder): Postdoctoral Fellow from the University of Michigan; PhD from the Chinese University of Hong Kong; former employee of Meta Reality Labs and SenseTime.

    Xin Zhou (Co-founder): Chief AI Architect at Baidu US; PhD from the University of Michigan; over 10 years of experience leading AI/genetic AI systems. Haoming (Co-founder): Postdoctoral Fellow at Peking University; PhD from Zhejiang University; former Baidu employee; published over 10 top-tier AI papers; multiple startups (Deep Pink Elephant, xPerception, the latter acquired by Baidu). Investment Rationale: 4D Labs integrates AI, 3D, and Web3 technologies, aiming to solve a fundamental bottleneck in the era of spatial computing: scalable, verifiable, high-quality 3D data. Its large-scale spatial models and 3D data generation/acquisition processes, combined with a tokenized data copyright framework, make it a pioneer in the field of 3D data infrastructure. With the accelerated development of digital twins and embodied AI, ownership-backed 3D data networks will become crucial—therefore, investing in 4D Labs is a timely and strategic move toward the foundational layer of the next-generation computing paradigm.

    AllScale: A Lightweight, Self-Managed New Bank for Cross-Border Business

    Project Team:

    Shawn Pang (CEO): Former Head of Payments at TikTok and Capital One; Serial Entrepreneur; Former Head of KuCoin's IPO.

    Leo Wang (COO): Former Executive at Amazon, IBM, and Scotiabank; Fintech and Infrastructure Builder.

    Leo Wang (COO): Former Executive at Amazon, IBM, and Scotiabank; Fintech and Infrastructure Builder.

    Alisha Li (CMO): Former Partner at Fenghou Capital, former Sales Director at HP/Dell/Sun. Khalil Lin (Compliance): Licensed to practice law in New York State; former member of Kraken and The Block. Investment Rationale: AllScale is building a new self-custodied stablecoin bank, simplifying payment, payroll, and invoicing processes for businesses in a compliant and intuitive way. Investing in AllScale is based on early signs of a high degree of product-market fit, as well as organic growth and robust user retention. AllScale combines the speed and transparency of stablecoins with a user experience that meets the operational and compliance needs of modern businesses, giving it a competitive edge in capturing market share.

    Advent: An AI-Driven Precision Gene Therapy Platform

    Project Team：

    Bonnie Zhu: Assistant Professor, Department of Chemical and Biological Engineering, Hong Kong University of Science and Technology; PhD from Stanford University.

    Kai Liu: Professor, Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, a leading figure in neuroscience.

    Kai Liu: Professor, Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, a leading figure in neuroscience.

    Kai Liu: Professor, Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, a leading figure in neuroscience.

    ... Yuan Yao: PhD in Bioengineering from Stanford University, currently Director of Sales and Marketing for Hemodialysis and Peritoneal Dialysis at Becton Dickinson. Investment Rationale: Advent is a leader in cell and gene therapy delivery, leveraging its proprietary viral protein datasets and a specially built AI-driven platform to reduce the discovery cycle of adeno-associated virus (AAV) capsids from months to weeks. Its internal high-throughput data, scalable and differentiated capsid intellectual property pipeline, and synthetic capsids that outperform natural serotypes create a strong technological moat in a rapidly growing market. With its strong team and clear technological differentiation, Advent represents a timely entry opportunity aligned with major precedents in the cell and gene therapy (CGT) market. AgriDynamics: Fruit-Harvesting Robots to Solve the Farm Labor Crisis

    Project Team：

    Dr. Zhaojian Li (CTO): Sequoia Distinguished Associate Professor at Michigan State University, leading the commercialization of a patented modular apple-harvesting robot developed with funding from the National Science Foundation and the U.S. Department of Agriculture.

    ...strong> Kyle Lammers (CEO): Dedicated to the research and development of apple-harvesting robots; recipient of the Michigan Agricultural Biotechnology Innovation Award, the American Society of Agricultural and Biological Engineers (ASABE) Best Student Presentation Award, and the Information Technology Services Community Best Paper Award. Investment Rationale: AgriDynamics has solved one of the most challenging cost and labor challenges in U.S. specialty agriculture—apple harvesting—through a patented, field-proven robotic platform. Labor costs account for approximately 70% of production costs in apple harvesting. The platform has been endorsed by major grower associations. Its modular, commercially viable, and defensible intellectual property gives it a significant first-mover advantage in a multi-billion dollar market. With a scalable roadmap, the company is poised to expand to other high-value crops and generate sustainable service/software revenue, thus becoming a core automated infrastructure for specialty agriculture.

    Bento.fun：The Social Layer of Predictive Markets

    Project Team：

    Abhitej Singh (CEO): Former Head of Growth at Persistence/pSTAKE; Co-founder of Cosmos India; Founder of Filament (deal value $100 million, funding $1.15 million)

    Pratyaksh Inani (CPO): Started quantitative trading at age 13; former treasury/product manager at Persistence; co-founder of Filament. Rajeeb Kumar (Engineer) – former Nethermind employee, IIT graduate, 6 years of DeFi/contract engineering experience, 10-time hackathon champion. Investment Rationale: Bento transforms daily fan discussions on Telegram, Twitter, and WhatsApp into micro-bets of $1-$10 in a simple and fast format, tapping into a casual fan base with huge trading volume potential. The founders have deep industry experience, targeting high-traffic global fan bases such as cricket and F1. As the native prediction market of BNB Chain, Bento aligns perfectly with our ecosystem strategy and drives broader on-chain participation through low-friction social betting.

    FingerDance: An AI Infrastructure for Sign Language Translation for the Deaf and Mute

    Project Team:

    Kevin Gong (Co-founder): Master of Business Analytics, National University of Singapore; Miracle Plus Researcher.

    Alex Xu (Co-founder): PhD in Computer Science, Peking University;

    Enzo Xu (Co-founder): Master of Computer and Information Science, University of Pennsylvania.

    Alex Xu (Co-founder): PhD in Computer Science, Peking University.

    Enzo Xu (Co-founder): Master of Computer and Information Science, University of Pennsylvania.

    Alex Xu (Co-founder): PhD in Computer Science, Peking University.

    Enzo Xu (Co-founder): Master of Computer and Information Science, University of Pennsylvania.

    ... Investment Rationale: Sign language is the primary language of the global deaf community, but access to information remains limited due to a severe shortage of sign language interpreters. FingerDance directly addresses this underserved market. By deploying a state-of-the-art Sign Language Large Scale Model (SL-LLM), FingerDance leverages artificial intelligence to provide real-time, scalable translation services—something human translation infrastructure cannot match. With five years of close collaboration with the deaf community, the team possesses a unique advantage that promises to revolutionize barrier-free communication. Frontrun: A professional trader wallet built for rapid discovery, due diligence, and execution.

    Project Team：

    Chris (Founder/CEO): Former Robinhood Crypto (built deposit/withdrawal functionality), Sui Founding Engineer (led an 8-person team to build the browser, SDK, RPC, and wallet with over 1 million users).

    ... Investment Rationale: Frontrun is a trader-centric wallet designed to address the clear pain points faced by high-frequency traders, enabling rapid discovery, due diligence, and seamless execution. Early organic growth and high retention rates validate the product's fit with its highly attractive initial target audience—a high-value group of traders in a multi-billion dollar market (Solana alone generates over $1 billion in annual fees). Its multi-chain graph and integration with the BNB Chain DeFi ecosystem make Frontrun a strategically important complement to the broader trading infrastructure.

    Help.fun: A cryptocurrency launch platform for non-profit tokens

    Project Team:

    Helena Everley (Co-founder): Stanford University undergraduate.

    Arash Attar (Co-founder and CTO): Developed one of the fastest Memecoin mobile trading applications and developed a high-performance sneaker bot.

    Project Team:

    Helena Everley (Co-founder): Stanford University undergraduate.

    Arash Attar (Co-founder and CTO): Developed one of the fastest Memecoin mobile trading applications and developed a high-performance sneaker bot.

    ... Investment Rationale: Help.fun is building a crypto-native fundraising platform that enables nonprofits and startups to issue charitable tokens in a fair and bot-free manner, addressing the long-standing integrity issues in on-chain fundraising. Its anti-sniper mechanisms (bait, puzzles, gas traps) ensure fair participation and provide a reliable alternative to predatory meme token issuances. It boasts strong early-stage execution and aligns well with the culture and liquidity of the BNB Chain. Help.fun serves as a scalable on-chain fundraising tool for mission-driven organizations.

    Hertzflow: A permissionless leveraged trading market for any asset

    Project Team:

    Vincent Shang (Co-founder): Over 10 years of experience in the cryptocurrency industry, one of the most influential CT KOLs.

    Hero (Co-founder and Head of Product): Previously involved in the development of CDP and Prime MPC wallets at Coinbase.

    Hero (Co-founder and Head of Product): Previously involved in the development of CDP and Prime MPC wallets at Coinbase.

    Soren (Technical Lead): A core developer of Mantle (early engineering team, built from scratch). Investment Rationale: Hertzflow aims to bridge the gap between the traditional retail derivatives market, with its $4.5 trillion daily trading volume, and the on-chain economy. Led by a team with deep expertise in traditional derivatives, the protocol is designed to attract a large user base of non-crypto-native traders. Hertzflow is a technologically mature product running within the BNB Chain ecosystem, perfectly combining the efficiency of permissionless on-chain settlement with a user experience tailored to the world's vast trading market. Manifolds: An AI-Powered 3D Spatial Controllable Video Generation Platform

    Project Team:

    Qingyuan Shan (CEO): Former Senior Vice President of Heywhale; Former Head of Corporate Strategy at Sun River; Bachelor of Science in Mathematics from the University at Buffalo, Master of Science in Applied Analytics from Columbia University.

    Yudong Tao (CTO): Former Research Data Scientist at Meta; Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering from Fudan University, PhD in Computer Engineering from the University of Miami.

    Yudong Tao (CTO): Former Research Data Scientist at Meta; Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering from Fudan University, PhD in Computer Engineering from the University of Miami.

    Yudong Tao (CTO): Former Research Data Scientist at Meta; Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering from Fudan University, PhD in Computer Engineering from the University of Miami.

    ... Naiyuan Huang (COO): Former Head of Sales and Marketing at Heywhale; alumnus of CEIBS; previously successfully developed China's second-largest AI talent community. Investment Rationale: Manifolds focuses on controllable 3D-aware AI video as an enabling layer for next-generation content creation and embodied AI applications. Its technological differentiation—including 3D space generation, physically-aware simulation, hybrid rendering, and sophisticated object/camera control—gives it an edge in high-growth verticals such as e-commerce visualization and real-world integrated AI systems. Strong early growth momentum (annual recurring revenue of $150,000 and 60 paying enterprise clients without paid marketing) demonstrates its mature development capabilities, scalable potential, and a strong alignment between the founders and the market.

    MeleeMon: A competitive mobile game using stablecoins for betting

    Project Team:

    Alex (CEO): Ranked among the top 500 Pokémon competitive players for four consecutive years; the founder's vision is highly aligned with the market.

    Jasper (CTO): Over 6 years of Unity and React development experience.

    Jasper (CTO): Over 6 years of Unity and React development experience.

    Investment Rationale:

    MeleeMon is a mobile skill-based monster battle game. Its team boasts a strong founder-market fit—they have received investment from YC, possess extensive game development experience (their former company, Gamebytes, had over 5 million users), have a strong reputation in the Pokémon esports arena, and have a clear understanding of blockchain-enhanced gaming economics. The project targets the large and mature Web2 gaming market while introducing a new competitive gaming loop on-chain. YZi Labs is committed to creating high-quality, engaging gaming experiences on BNB Chain, and MeleeMon, with its reliable execution capabilities, aligns perfectly with YZi Labs' strategy.

    Neomera BioLab: A drug discovery platform providing better non-opioid therapies for chronic pain and major chronic diseases.

    Neomera BioLab: A drug discovery platform providing better non-opioid therapies for chronic pain and major chronic diseases.

    Project Team：

    Rebecca Miao (CEO): Over 15 years of experience in drug development; former Chief Scientific Officer of AKSO Bio; led multiple FDA-approved INDs; facilitated a $75 million pharmaceutical collaboration; holds over 200 patents; Postdoctoral Fellow at Stanford University.

    Jimmy Xu (CTO): Bachelor of Science from Johns Hopkins University; PhD/Postdoctoral Fellow in Bioengineering from Stanford University; Expert in drug screening optimization and biomedical artificial intelligence.

    Jimmy Xu (CTO): Bachelor of Science from Johns Hopkins University; PhD/Postdoctoral Fellow in Bioengineering from Stanford University; Expert in drug screening optimization and biomedical artificial intelligence.

    ... Yi Lu (CFO): A former investor and serial entrepreneur, she has developed novel pain management therapies from concept to trial, earning recognition and awards from top international publications. She holds an MBA from Stanford University and previously worked as a partner at Bain Capital and Madron Capital. Investment Rationale: Neomera is dedicated to addressing the urgent need for non-addictive analgesics for over 1 billion chronic pain sufferers worldwide. Its biology-led, AI-accelerated discovery platform offers significant advantages: it generates proprietary, highly relevant internal data and successfully targets indications that traditional methods cannot address. This tightly integrated wet lab + machine learning cycle fundamentally shortens experimental cycles by up to 80%, thus building a scalable and differentiated engine for rapid drug development for refractory chronic diseases.

    Sats Terminal: Bitcoin Native Liquidity Protocol

    Project Team：

    Stan Havryliuk (CEO): Previously held senior leadership positions at Bitcoin.com, ZondaCrypto, and Ambisafe; has expanded multiple teams from 5 to 50 people.

    Saturn Labs: 10%+ Yield, Backed by Bitcoin Credit

    Project Team:

    Kevin Li (Co-founder & CEO): Co-founder of ParaFi; Former Head of Artemis DAT

    Sebastian Melendez (Co-founder & CTO): Co-founder, Former Head of Artemis Stablecoins

    Predict.fun: Prediction Markets that Solve Capital Inefficiency by Leveraging DeFi-Enhanced Liquidity

    Project Team：

    Ding: Previously worked in consulting/investment banking; joined Binance in 2017 and built the research and IPO team; led Binance Launchpad (2019); founded PancakeSwap (2020); co-founded Predict.fun (2024). Investment Rationale: Predict.fun has created a new type of mainstream prediction market by transforming event trading into a yield-generating, leverage-enabled, and self-custodial financial foundation, allowing users to fully experience decentralized finance (DeFi) while participating. By integrating DeFi-native yields, a seamless on-chain user experience (AA, gas-free trading), and composability, the protocol lowers the barrier to adoption and positions prediction markets as easy-to-use and highly practical products for a broader non-crypto-native user base.

    Trellis Robotics: AI-powered soft robotics platform unlocking hard-to-reach industrial assets

    Project Team：

    Yimeng Qin (CEO): PhD candidate in Mechanical Engineering at Stanford University; Lead developer of core vine robotics technology (scheduled to complete studies in March 2026).

    ... Elvy Yao (COO): Master of Mechanical Engineering, Stanford University (graduating December 2025); a major contributor to the Trellis platform's sensor housing, steering, and mechatronics. William Heap (Technical Lead): PhD candidate in Mechanical Engineering, Stanford University (full-time after 2028); has been researching vine robotic systems at UC Santa Barbara and Stanford University since 2019. Investment Rationale: Trellis Robotics is dedicated to solving one of the most costly and dangerous challenges in industrial operations: the inspection of confined space infrastructure. Its unique vine robotic platform provides unparalleled precision, coverage, and safety compared to human inspectors and existing tools, creating a clear competitive advantage in a large and underserved market. Early validation—including pilot projects with Dow Chemical and Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory, as well as strong interest from companies such as BASF, Bayer, and Chevron—demonstrates strong market demand and a reliable path to enterprise-level applications.

data.title: Children Can Now Earn Interest on Crypto Through Binance Junior as Parents Retain Full Account Control
data.description:
Binance Junior Sparks Debate Over Crypto Savings For Children

Families are becoming a new focus in the crypto world as Binance introduces a supervised savings app designed for users as young as six.

The product, Binance Junior, brings digital assets into a space traditionally occupied by bank-linked custodial accounts, raising fresh discussion about how early financial education should begin — and how far crypto firms should go in courting young users.

Introducing Binance Junior, a parent-controlled app and sub-account for kids and teens.

Build family-focused crypto savings and prepare your child for a future empowered by crypto.

Try it now 👉 https://t.co/q4Y50PvApypic.twitter.com/O1R2yZ4vVE

— Binance (@binance) December 3, 2025

A New Family-Focused Crypto Model From Binance

Binance Junior operates as a controlled sub-account fully tied to a parent or guardian’s verified Binance profile, with all assets legally owned and managed by the adult.

Parents can fund the junior account through their main Binance wallets or via on-chain transfers, allocate savings, set spending rules, and approve all movements of funds.

Up to five junior accounts can be linked to a single parent.

Children cannot trade, use derivatives, withdraw crypto on-chain, or move funds to adult users outside the family.

Transfers between Junior accounts are capped at 400 dollars per day.

In selected markets, the app offers a Junior Flexible Simple Earn feature that lets young users earn interest on their savings.

Source: Binance

Teenagers aged 13 and above can use Binance Pay to send and receive crypto from parents or other junior profiles, subject to regional age rules and parental controls.

Yi He, Binance’s co-founder, positioned the launch as part of a wider shift toward long-term savings habits, saying:

“Today, parents can take the first steps to prepare for their children’s financial future and equip them for the future financial landscape.”

Where Can Families Use It And How Do Age Rules Differ?

Global availability remains patchy.

Local digital consent laws determine whether minors can access the app, and Binance says some jurisdictions may update restrictions over time.

Age thresholds vary widely: users must be at least 14 in Austria, Spain and South Korea, and 16 in Germany and Brazil.

France and Greece set the minimum at 15.

Source: Binance

These variations create a staggered rollout, unlike exchanges such as Coinbase and Gemini, which ban all under-18 users.

The fragmented regulatory approach means parents must refer to Binance’s official guidance to confirm eligibility and local restrictions before opening a junior account.

Why Binance Says Early Exposure Matters

Alongside the app, Binance has published an illustrated book, ABC’s of Crypto, to help younger readers understand digital assets, security basics and blockchain concepts.

Binance just released the “ABC’s of Crypto” and I’ve been going through it for a while.
Really like how it breaks down the basics in a simple, fun way as crypto becomes part of everyday finance.

Here are a few of my favourite parts from the book (photos below) 👇
It actually… pic.twitter.com/RLMbOzABBX

— Crypto Verse (@CryptoVerse_Co) December 3, 2025

The company frames the entire initiative as a financial literacy programme rather than a trading pathway, saying it wants to help children develop saving habits as crypto becomes more integrated into mainstream finance.

The platform’s Minor Mode restricts the interface to simple balance viewing and savings features for younger users, while older teens receive controlled access based on parental preferences.

Why Critics Say The Move Is Risky

The debut has divided the crypto community.

Supporters believe the controlled environment presents a practical way for families to introduce financial concepts early, mirroring how traditional custodial accounts work.

But critics argue that involving children in volatile markets is inappropriate, regardless of controls.

Some commentators called the initiative “crazy and irresponsible,” while others warned that minors could become “exit liquidity” in a market where Bitcoin recently swung from roughly 125,000 dollars to around 95,000 dollars.

Whats with this trend of giving stocks to new borns (Trump Accounts) and Binance junior for children.

Things are getting so bad that we need the next generation for our exit liquidity

— Pump Truck (@TruckPump) December 3, 2025

The mixed response highlights lingering concerns about whether crypto platforms should design youth-focused products at all, even when trading features are removed.

How Binance Junior Fits Into A Changing Market

Binance’s scale, reaching more than 290 million users globally, means its approach carries influence far beyond earlier youth-focused experiments by smaller platforms.

Its decision to allow supervised participation could set a new precedent for exchanges seeking to reach younger demographics while navigating increasingly strict marketing and access rules.

Smaller companies like Stack in Seattle have explored similar ideas for teenagers, but none match Binance’s reach or regulatory exposure.

As jurisdictions tighten rules around digital finance, the reception to Binance Junior may inform whether such services expand or face new limits.

Is The Society Ready For Crypto Kids

Families today are managing money across apps before children even reach secondary school, and Binance Junior pushes this reality further by treating crypto like any other household saving tool.

Coinlive sees this moment as a test of how society defines financial readiness in an era where digital money is no longer niche.

If future generations grow up viewing crypto as casually as fiat, it raises deeper questions about responsibility — not just for parents, but for platforms shaping those early experiences.

The debate now is less about technology and more about how young is too young when the lines between traditional finance and digital assets continue to blur.

data.title: DAT Company: A Concept in Transition
data.description:
Author: James Butterfill, Source: CoinShares Research, Translated by: Shaw & Jinse Finance

Over the past two years, Digital Asset Treasury (DAT) companies have become one of the most watched sectors in the cryptocurrency market. As CoinShares operates a blockchain stock index, this is also a topic we often discuss in depth. With the rapid growth of the DAT sector and its reputation, its positioning has become increasingly blurred. The recent market correction has put pressure on some large DATs, so it is necessary to re-examine the original intentions and development history of DATs, and what the recent market downturn and shrinking net asset value mean for their future development.

The Original Intentions and Core Positioning of DATs

To understand the current situation, we need to review the original motivations for creating DATs. The core objective of DATs is to serve multinational companies with multi-currency revenue streams that need to manage funds and foreign exchange risks. For these companies, Bitcoin offers a highly attractive hedging tool, effectively mitigating the risks of quantitative easing, rising government debt, and long-term currency devaluation. Including Bitcoin on their balance sheets is not speculation, but a financial management strategy, as evidenced by Strategy's initial announcement in August 2020. Furthermore, this aligns with the growing interest in distributed ledger technology among many enterprises and the potential efficiency gains from integrating blockchain infrastructure into existing operations. Strictly speaking, DAT simply refers to companies that hold Bitcoin or other crypto assets on their balance sheets. The market has gradually established an implicit threshold: companies must hold a significant proportion of cryptocurrency (typically above 40% of net assets) to be classified as DAT. As cryptocurrency purchases accelerate and valuations soar, they often obscure a company's core business. Strategy is a prime example: what was initially intended as a diversification initiative has effectively become a leveraged Bitcoin investment vehicle. Many new entrants followed suit, issuing shares not to grow their businesses, but to accumulate more digital assets. However, over time, this diminished interest and funding in the sector, raising questions about the sustainability of these strategies. Subsequently, these companies began utilizing every available financing channel to rapidly increase their cryptocurrency holdings, hoping that rising prices would compensate for insufficient growth in their core businesses. The recent cryptocurrency market correction exposed these structural flaws. Several factors contributed to this decline, including a lack of robust operational support for their cash management strategies, the diversion of funds to other blockchain-related stock investments (such as mining), and the overall decline in cryptocurrency prices. For many such companies, their traditional businesses were unprofitable, which could create selling pressure, although this pressure was usually relatively small relative to their digital asset holdings. Bitmine (BMNR) is a prime example: in its last fiscal quarter, the company's operating cash outflow was a mere $5 million, while its Ethereum (ETH) reserves were worth over $10 billion. Metaplanet, headquartered in Japan, also has negligible operating cash flow compared to its $2.7 billion Bitcoin holdings. On the other hand, dividend and interest payments can lead to more pressing selling demand, especially when liquid fiat currency resources are scarce. However, most DATs are financed through stock issuances, resulting in relatively low debt burdens. The sole exception is Strategy, which has $8.2 billion in outstanding debt and has issued $7 billion in dividend-paying preferred stock. Strategy's debt generates approximately $800 million in cash flow commitments annually, which the company has funded through further financing. To allay concerns about its solvency, Strategy again invoked its market offering (ATM) mechanism, issuing $1.4 billion as reserves to pay preferred stock dividends and interest. DAT companies will use every available means to avoid selling their digital asset reserves, and so far, the major DAT companies we follow have not made any large-scale sales this year. However, the question remains: what happens when mNAV (market capitalization to net asset value) falls below 1? Contrary to popular belief, this scenario is not entirely hopeless. Companies holding cryptocurrencies can still increase their per-share cryptocurrency holdings by reversing their accumulation strategies. In this scenario, the company would sell cryptocurrency and buy back shares, thereby increasing the per-share token holdings. While this makes sense, we believe that management teams striving to scale their businesses are unlikely to actively reduce their cryptocurrency holdings, potentially leading to stagnant growth in their holdings until the funding environment improves. Finally, companies trading below their cryptocurrency net asset value could become attractive acquisition targets, as well-funded companies might see them as a way to acquire digital assets below cost. Has the DAT bubble burst? In many ways, yes. In the summer of 2025, many of these companies were trading at 3, 5, or even 10 times their net asset value, but now they are hovering around 1 times or even lower. The market will then diverge: either a price drop triggers a disorderly sell-off, leading to a market crash; or companies continue to hold their assets, waiting for prices to recover. We lean towards the latter, especially given the improving macroeconomic backdrop and the potential for a December rate cut, which will provide broader support for the cryptocurrency market. However, in the long run, the DAT model needs to evolve. Investors will have less tolerance for equity dilution and highly concentrated assets lacking substantial revenue streams. The original intention of quality companies to diversify fiat currency risk has been obscured by companies heavily reliant on the public stock market to build massive assets rather than develop genuine businesses, undermining the credibility of the entire industry. Encouragingly, a number of stronger companies are beginning to include Bitcoin on their balance sheets for strategic purposes. However, by the current informal definition, these companies should not be considered DATs at all. Ironically, companies that best fit the original intent of currency hedging/foreign exchange management strategies, such as Tesla, Trump Media Group, and Block Inc., are currently excluded from this label. The Future Direction of the DAT Concept The bursting of the DAT bubble does not mean the end of the DAT concept. On the contrary, we expect a market readjustment. Investors will increasingly differentiate between the following: Speculative DATs: Core business is secondary; their value depends almost entirely on token holdings. Asset Reserve-Oriented DATs: Use Bitcoin or other digital assets as part of a rigorous foreign exchange and cash reserve strategy. Token Investment Firms: Businesses holding diversified token portfolios, potentially resembling closed-end funds rather than traditional companies. Strategic Firms: Adding Bitcoin to their balance sheets as a macro hedging tool, but not seeking to be classified as DATs. The past year's experience has shown that the term "DAT" can represent everything or nothing at the same time; therefore, the industry is moving towards clearer classifications. Conclusion The birth of DAT stemmed from a sound concept: **corporations diversify their financial reserves by shifting from fiat currency to digital assets.** However, the rapid expansion of token reserves, equity dilution, and the relentless pursuit of increasing the number of tokens per share undermined this initial intention. **With the bursting of the bubble, the market is reassessing which companies truly embody the DAT model and which are merely riding the wave.** The future of DAT lies in returning to fundamental principles: **rigorous financial management, a reliable business model, and a realistic expectation of the role of digital assets on the corporate balance sheet.** The next generation of DAT companies will be closer to the originally envisioned model: stable, globalized operations, and the strategic rather than speculative use of digital assets.

data.title: Bitwise: Strategy will never sell its Bitcoin holdings.
data.description:
Author: Matt Hougan, Chief Investment Officer of Bitwise; Translated by: Jinse Finance

There are many things to worry about in the cryptocurrency space, but Michael Saylor and Strategy's sale of Bitcoin is definitely not one of them.

My inbox is overflowing with questions about Bitcoin treasury company Strategy (stock code: MSTR, formerly MicroStrategy). Specifically, people mainly want to know two things:

  1. Will it be removed from the MSCI index, leading to a forced sell-off of the stock?

  2. Will it be forced to sell its Bitcoin holdings?

Let's answer them one by one.

Let's answer them one by one.

On October 10th, MSCI announced it was considering removing digital asset treasury companies (DATs) like Strategy from its investable indices. This move has significant implications—nearly $17 trillion in global assets are benchmarked against these indices. JPMorgan estimates that if Strategy is removed from the benchmark index, index funds could be forced to sell up to $2.8 billion worth of MSTR shares. You might wonder: Why would MSCI do this? Their view is that digital asset treasury companies like MSTR are closer to holding companies than operating companies. MSCI's investable indices typically do not include holding companies such as real estate investment trusts (REITs), and since many digital asset treasury companies focus solely on buying and holding crypto assets, MSCI believes they should not be included in the indices. After consulting with clients, MSCI will announce its final decision on January 15th. I cannot predict MSCI's final decision. As a seasoned index professional (I spent 10 years editing the academic journal *The Journal of Indexes*), I believe both outcomes are possible. Michael Thaler and others have strongly countered that MSTR is purely an operating company with a robust software business and complex financial engineering around Bitcoin—a reasonable view, and also my opinion of its business. However, this is not a done deal, and some institutions may lean towards supporting MSCI's proposal. Given the controversial nature of digital asset treasury companies and MSCI's current tendency to exclude such companies, I estimate there is at least a 75% probability that Strategy will be removed from the index. However, I **do not believe that removal from the index will have a significant impact on the stock**. While a $2.8 billion sell-off seems massive, based on my years of experience observing index component adjustments, the actual impact of such events is usually smaller than expected, and the market digests it in advance. For example, when MSTR was included in the Nasdaq 100 index last December, funds tracking the index were required to buy $2.1 billion worth of stocks, but its share price barely fluctuated. I believe that the slight decline in MSTR's share price since October 11th is partly due to the market already priced in the expectation of its removal from the index. However, it seems unlikely that the share price will experience significant volatility regardless of MSCI's final decision. In the long run, MSTR's value depends on the effectiveness of its strategic execution, not on whether index funds are forced to hold its shares. Another question is whether MSTR will sell its Bitcoin holdings. Specifically, the market's chain of concern is as follows: MSTR was removed from the MSCI index; its stock price plummeted, falling significantly below its net asset value (NAV); therefore, MSTR was forced to sell Bitcoin. This logic seems reasonable, but unfortunately, it's completely wrong for short sellers. MSTR's stock price falling below its NAV does not trigger any mandatory Bitcoin selling clauses—you can check the details and calculate it yourself. MSTR's debt-related obligations are primarily twofold: **approximately $800 million in annual interest payments, and the conversion or renewal of certain debt instruments upon maturity**. Interest payments are not a near-term concern—the company currently holds $1.4 billion in cash, easily covering interest expenses for a year and a half. Similarly, debt conversion is not a near-term issue. The first debt instrument matures in February 2027 and is only about $1 billion, a drop in the ocean compared to the company's $60 billion in Bitcoin holdings. If MSTR's stock price continues to decline, will insiders pressure to sell Bitcoin? Unlikely. Michael Thaler himself holds 42% of the voting shares, and you'd be hard-pressed to find someone more confident in Bitcoin's long-term value than him. He didn't sell any Bitcoin when MSTR stock last traded at a discount in 2022. I understand why short sellers are so keen to promote MSTR's "death spiral" theory—if MSTR were forced to sell its entire $60 billion worth of Bitcoin in one go, it would indeed be catastrophic for the Bitcoin market (equivalent to two years' worth of inflows into a Bitcoin ETF). However, considering the company doesn't have any debt maturing until 2027 and has sufficient cash to cover foreseeable interest expenses, I don't think this will happen. Furthermore, we should look at the issue from a broader perspective: as of this writing, Bitcoin is priced at approximately $92,000, down 27% from its all-time high, but still 24% higher than Strategy's average purchase price ($74,436)—the so-called "death spiral" simply doesn't hold water.

Conclusion

If you want to focus on what's truly worrying about the cryptocurrency space, consider the following: For example, I'm slightly concerned about the slow progress of market structure-related legislation in Congress (though I think the pace will pick up as the government returns to normal operations); I'm concerned that some smaller, poorly managed digital asset treasury companies may go bankrupt; and I believe that digital asset treasury companies won't be buying large amounts of Bitcoin in 2026, which will lead to the disappearance of a significant source of recent demand.

Conclusion

If you want to focus on what's truly worrying about the cryptocurrency space, consider the following: For example, I'm slightly concerned about the slow progress of market structure-related legislation in Congress (though I think the pace will pick up as the government returns to normal operations); I'm worried that some smaller, poorly managed digital asset treasury companies may go bankrupt; and I don't think that digital asset treasury companies will be buying large amounts of Bitcoin in 2026, which will lead to the disappearance of a significant source of recent demand.

But as far as Strategy is concerned: There's no need to worry about the impact of MSCI's decision on its stock price—the impact is smaller than most people expect and has likely already been priced in by the market; there's no reasonable mechanism in the short term to force it to sell Bitcoin—that's simply not going to happen. A firm belief in Bitcoin comes at a price: it requires calm and patience when market volatility occurs. No one understands this better than Thaler and Strategy—because they also understand the other side of the coin: in the long run, this patience will ultimately pay off.

data.title: Markets warn: Nominating Hassett as Fed Chair could trigger a storm of interest rate cuts.
data.description:
Author: Xiao Yanyan, Jinshi Data

Bond investors have informed the U.S. Treasury Department of their concerns regarding Kevin Hassett's potential nomination as Federal Reserve Chairman, fearing he might aggressively cut interest rates to appease Trump.

According to multiple sources familiar with the matter, Treasury officials solicited feedback on Hassett and other candidates during one-on-one conversations with executives from major Wall Street banks, asset management giants, and other key players in the U.S. bond market.

These discussions occurred in November, before Treasury Secretary Bessant initiated the second round of interviews to find a successor to Powell. The Treasury Department stated that it "regularly communicates with various market participants and investors regarding important developments and dynamics in the U.S. Treasury market and the broader financial markets."

The White House added, "In discussions with key stakeholders, the market's expectations for the potential asset outcomes of the five potential Federal Reserve Chair nominees are extremely narrow." As the White House's chief economic officer, Hassett has emerged as a leading candidate for the position in recent weeks, after Trump and Bessett narrowed the shortlist of potential candidates from an initial 11. Trump said on Tuesday that he plans to announce his nominee for Federal Reserve Chair "early" next year, hinting that Hassett is a "potential" contender. The dollar briefly fell after he mentioned Hassett. The White House told the Financial Times, "The President will continue to nominate the most qualified person for the federal government, and any discussion of potential nominees before the President's own announcement is pointless speculation." The Treasury Department added that it "believes [Trump's] choice will serve the American people well." Market participants' concerns about Hassett reflect broader Wall Street anxiety about a change in Federal Reserve leadership as Trump prepares to nominate a new central bank governor. Some veteran bond market participants prefer other candidates, such as Rick Rieder of BlackRock and Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller, who are considered more independent and less connected to Trump than Hassett. Several market participants who have spoken with the Treasury Department have expressed concern about Hassett's close relationship with Trump. Trump has consistently insisted on significant interest rate cuts and has called Powell a "stubborn mule" for the central bank's only modest reductions in borrowing costs this year. According to three people familiar with the matter, bankers and investors are worried that Hassett might advocate for indiscriminate rate cuts even if inflation persists above the Fed's 2% target. “Nobody wants to experience a ‘Truss shock,’” said one market participant, referring to the turmoil in the UK bond market in 2022 triggered by then-Prime Minister Liz Truss’s unfunded tax cut plan. With US inflation likely to rise next year, the prospect of a dovish Federal Reserve chair is seen as particularly worrying by large bond managers. The Fed’s preferred inflation gauge recorded 2.7% in August. One market participant said that the combination of loose monetary policy and higher inflation could trigger a sell-off in long-term government bonds. Sources added that some market participants also do not believe Hassett can win the support of the divided Federal Reserve Board and build consensus on interest rate decisions. According to two people familiar with the matter, those involved in these conversations included members of the Wall Street bond giants who make up the Treasury Borrowing Advisory Committee (TBAC), which advises Bessett on market and issuance issues. These individuals said that when Hassett, an economist whose career has focused on tax policy research, met with the TBAC earlier this year, he spent little time talking about markets, instead promoting key White House issues, including discussions of Mexican drug cartels. A Washington insider, Hassett served as a senior economic advisor for the presidential campaigns of John McCain, George W. Bush, and Mitt Romney before joining the White House as chairman of the Council of Economic Advisers during Trump's first term. He also worked at the conservative think tank American Enterprise Institute and at the Federal Reserve, where former Fed staff remember him as ambitious. Robert Tetlow, a former senior policy advisor at the Federal Reserve who recently left the position, described Hassett as “intelligent, eloquent, and confident.” However, there are widespread concerns that his close relationship with Trump threatens the Fed’s independence, as Trump has repeatedly attacked the central bank over the past year. Claudia Sahm, a former Fed economist and now chief economist at New Century Advisors, stated, “Kevin Hassett is fully capable of being Fed chairman. The question is, which one will take over? The Kevin Hassett who was actively involved in the Trump administration? Or the Kevin Hassett as an independent economist?” John Stopford, head of multi-income at Ninety One, added, “I think the market sees him as Trump’s puppet, which erodes the Fed’s credibility to some extent.”

data.title: OpenAI will acquire Neptune to enhance its AI model training and monitoring capabilities.
data.description:
OpenAI has reached a definitive agreement to acquire Neptune, a startup that provides monitoring and debugging tools for AI companies during model training.

According to a blog post, Neptune CEO Piotr Niedźwiedź revealed that Neptune and OpenAI had previously collaborated on a metrics dashboard designed to assist teams building foundational models. He stated that the acquisition will allow the two companies to "work more closely together."

Nedźwiedź also indicated that the startup will gradually cease providing external services over the next few months. Specific terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

"Neptune has built a fast and accurate system that enables researchers to analyze complex training workflows," said Jakub Pachocki, Chief Scientist at OpenAI, in a statement. "We plan to work closely with them to deeply integrate their tools into our training technology stack to enhance our insights into the model learning process."

OpenAI has completed several acquisitions this year. In October, the company acquired a small UI startup called Software Applications Incorporated for an undisclosed amount; in September, it acquired product development platform Statsig for $1.1 billion (approximately RMB 7.786 billion at the current exchange rate); and in May, it acquired io, an AI hardware startup founded by Jony Ive, for over $6 billion (approximately RMB 42.469 billion at the current exchange rate).

According to Neptune's official website, the company has previously raised over $18 million (approximately RMB 127 million) from investors including Almaz Capital and TDJ Pitango Ventures. Currently, the transaction between Neptune and OpenAI is still subject to standard closing conditions.

Nedzwitzki stated, "I am deeply grateful to our customers, investors, co-founders, and colleagues; their support has made this journey possible. It has already been an unforgettable experience, but I believe this is just the beginning."

data.title: Liang Fengyi Seeks Change Amidst Risks: How Can the Digital Asset Industry Innovate and Advance?
data.description:
Author: Zhang Feng

On December 3, 2025, Ms. Leung Fung-yee, Chief Executive Officer of the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC), delivered a keynote speech at the 4th ASEAN Plus Three Economic Cooperation and Financial Stability Forum. Her speech, centered on "Reconstructing Finance - Regulation Seeking Progress Amidst Risks in a Changing Era," systematically elaborated on the profound changes currently facing the financial market, the accompanying risks, and the corresponding strategies that regulatory agencies and market participants should adopt.

Against the backdrop of the global digital wave, the restructuring of the geopolitical landscape, and the empowerment of finance by technology, her speech not only set the tone for financial regulation in Hong Kong and even Asia, but also provided a clear roadmap for the standardized development of emerging fields such as digital assets.

In the context of the digital wave sweeping the globe, the reshaping of the geopolitical landscape, and the empowerment of finance by technology, her speech not only set the tone for financial regulation in Hong Kong and even Asia, but also provided a clear roadmap for the standardized development of emerging fields such as digital assets.

data.title: The illusion of liquidity is shattering – an in-depth review in December 2025.
data.description:
I. MicroStrategy's $6.6 Billion Discount: The First Shutdown of the Bitcoin Perpetual Motion Machine

Currently, MSTR holds 650,000 Bitcoins, with a fair value of $55.6 billion, but its total market capitalization is only $49 billion, a discount of $6.6 billion (11.9%), far exceeding the so-called "$1 billion". This is the first sustained and significant NAV discount since Thaler launched the Bitcoin Treasury Strategy in 2020. The mNAV ratio has fallen to 0.88, just one step away from the MSCI index removal threshold. Once removed, passive funds will sell approximately $2.8 billion worth of stocks, officially starting the death spiral. In November, $1.44 billion of preferred stock was issued to fill the interest gap, further diluting equity.

The bitter fruit of high beta finally borne fruit when Bitcoin plummeted from $108,000 to $85,500. Some still stubbornly insist, "I won't sell even if Bitcoin drops to $1." But the market voted with its feet: stock prices have fallen 41% this year, while Bitcoin has only dropped 7.2%. Short-term support is at $140, and resistance is at $180; in the long term, only if Bitcoin returns to above $100,000 can the premium myth be restarted.

II. The 30-Year End of Japanese Government Bonds: Either a Currency Crisis or a Debt Crisis

The 10-year JGB yield is 1.88%, and the 30-year yield is 3.20%, both marking the fastest monthly increases since 2008.

The central bank governor's speech on December 1st was interpreted as "a December rate hike is a foregone conclusion," with the market pricing in an 82% probability of a rate hike. The yen surged, with USD/JPY briefly falling below 145. This is a combined result of a 260% debt-to-GDP ratio, election-related fiscal expansion, and Trump's tariff threats. Over the past week, the yen and Bitcoin have shown a rare positive correlation (correlation coefficient 0.85), essentially due to the appreciation of the world's largest carry trade currency, forcing arbitrageurs to close their positions and "stealing" liquidity. Whenever similar scenarios occur, Bitcoin almost simultaneously falls by 1.2%. III. The Fed's $29.4 Billion Overnight Repurchase: Systemic Undercurrents Are Already Surgeing. The $29.4 billion injection on November 30th was the second largest single-day injection since 2020. SOFR jumped to 5.38%, and the reserve requirement ratio is approaching the 8.7% red line during the repo crisis in September 2019. Although QT officially ended on December 1, banks' active lending indicates that cracks have appeared in the funding situation. On the surface, "everything is fine," but underneath, holes are already being patched. Institutional bond holdings have fallen to 15%, completely coinciding with the eve of the dot-com bubble and the subprime mortgage crisis. The bid-to-cover ratio for 20-year and 30-year US Treasury bonds in November auctions fell below 2.3 times, and tail risks continue to amplify. No one is buying bonds, which means that when risk aversion reverses again, yields will jump, and that will be the starting point of a systemic event.

V. Asset Rotation and Key Technical Levels

  • The S&P 500, excluding the "Big Seven," hits a record high, and its breadth is actually improving

  • The Nasdaq has risen for seven consecutive months, but the average return over the next three months after the eighth month is only 0.8%, with a significant increase in volatility

  • VII. December's Seasonal Scenario and the Real Test in 2026

    December will likely see a period of consolidation in the first week, digesting the overheated Thanksgiving sentiment. The two weeks before Christmas will likely see a year-end sprint, with the S&P 500 likely reaching the $6,850-$7,000 range, and Bitcoin likely recovering to around $100,000. However, 2026 will be the real test: Japan's interest rate hike, the Fed's potential restart of QT, the out-of-control US fiscal deficit, and Trump's tariffs—these are all ticking time bombs. Conclusion The market in December 2025 is not the end of the bull market, nor the beginning of the bear market, but rather "the illusion of liquidity in the final stage of the bull market is being gradually stripped away." All the glaring signals—MSTR discount, yen appreciation, Fed repo, tail risk in US Treasury auctions, and zero bond allocation—are pointing to the same fact: the global financial system is warming up for even more violent volatility in 2026.

data.title: Markets warn the US Treasury: Nominating Hassett as Federal Reserve Chairman could trigger a storm of interest rate cuts.

data.title: Ethereum mainnet activated Fusaka upgrade; Polymarket launches US version of app.
data.description:
Headline

▌Ethereum Mainnet Activates Fusaka Upgrade, Officially Entering a Two-Hard-Forks-a-Year Rhythm

Ethereum's 17th major upgrade, "Fusaka," was officially activated on the mainnet at epoch 411392 (approximately 5:50 AM Beijing time on Thursday), only about 7 months after the Pectra upgrade in May. This upgrade not only brings several UX and scaling improvements but also marks Ethereum's official entry into an accelerated development rhythm of "two hard forks per year." Consensys stated that Ethereum researchers will aim for two upgrades per year in the future. Since the Merge in 2022, Ethereum has typically only undergone one major update per year, namely Shapella (2023), Dencun (2024), and Pectra (2025).

... Fusaka introduced several key technical changes, including the new sampling technology "PeerDAS," which impacts data availability and pricing. This upgrade includes 9 core EIPs and 4 supporting EIPs, making it one of the largest Ethereum upgrades to date by some statistical standards. Polymarket launched its US app on the X platform, allowing US residents to trade on Polymarket for the first time. Polymarket will initially launch a sports section, followed by market prediction features covering all sectors. As of press time, according to CoinGecko data: BTC price is $93,572.98, a 24-hour change of +2.0%; ETH price is $3,183.04, a 24-hour change of +5.7%; BNB price is $918.80, a 24-hour change of +4.5%. SOL price is $144.92, up 4.3% in the last 24 hours; DOGE price is $0.1521, up 3.7% in the last 24 hours; XRP price is $2.20, up 1.8% in the last 24 hours; TRX price is $0.2806, down 0.2% in the last 24 hours. WLFI price is $0.1609, a 24-hour change of +0.3%; HYPE price is $34.80, a 24-hour change of +1.9%. Policy: ▌SEC Chairman: Bitcoin Market Structure Bill Imminent Passage. According to The Bitcoin Historian, U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) Chairman Paul Atkins stated in a live interview with Fox News that the Bitcoin market structure bill is imminent passage.

▌SEC Chairman: SEC Needs to Continue Pushing Forward with Tokenization

According to DEGEN NEWS, U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission Chairman Paul Atkins stated that the agency needs to continue pushing forward with tokenization.

▌US SEC Suspends Approval of Highly Leveraged ETFs, Concerned About Exceeding Risk Limits

The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has issued a series of warning letters to some of the country's most prolific providers of highly leveraged exchange-traded funds (ETFs), effectively halting the launch of products designed to offer two or three times the daily returns on stocks and commodities. In nine nearly identical letters released Tuesday, the SEC informed companies including Direxion, ProShares, and Tidal that it would not proceed with reviews of proposed offerings until key issues were resolved. The regulator's core concern is that these funds' risk exposure may exceed the SEC's limits on the risks a fund can take relative to its assets.

... These letters instructed fund managers to either modify their investment strategies or formally withdraw their applications. According to Cointelegraph, the UK has passed a bill related to cryptocurrency property rights, which has been approved by the Crown—legally recognizing cryptocurrencies, stablecoins, and other crypto assets as personal property, subject to relevant property laws. According to an article in Caijing magazine, the computer Qian Zhimin took with him when he fled contained a cryptocurrency wallet. After several searches by British police, multiple mobile phones, computers, and USB drives were seized. One of the USB drives contained photos of pages from Qian Zhimin's notebook, which appeared to have been torn off before being seized by the police. The page photo shows the black computer's login password and several mnemonic phrases, such as "number + love," along with suggestive information about numbers, making the discovery of Bitcoin possible. According to *Caijing*, the Supreme People's Court held its eighth national criminal trial work conference last month, mentioning the improvement of judicial rules and focusing on new issues such as the handling of virtual currencies involved in cases and the authentication of electronic data evidence.

Blockchain Applications

▌Near Protocol Launches NEAR AI Cloud Service and Private Chat Function

Near Protocol announced the launch of two new products—NEAR AI Cloud service and Private Chat function. In NEAR AI Cloud service, each request runs within Intel TDX and NVIDIA confidential computing hardware, data is processed in a sealed and isolated environment, and each inference generates encrypted proof to verify that the model ran the expected code.

BlackRock: US Debt Growth Will Drive Cryptocurrency Rise

BlackRock released its 2026 outlook. Leaving aside its pessimistic assessment of US bonds and the world's largest economy, the report essentially outlines an optimistic roadmap for accelerated institutional adoption of cryptocurrencies. The report points out that US federal debt will surpass $38 trillion, market fragility will increase, and traditional hedging tools will gradually become ineffective. In this environment, Wall Street giants will turn to digital assets as alternative investments more quickly. Samara Cohen, BlackRock's Global Head of Markets Development, stated that stablecoins have "moved out of the niche" and are becoming a key bridge between traditional finance and digital liquidity.

... Charles Schwab: Bitcoin and Ethereum Trading Services to be Available in Early 2026 Charles Schwab announced that it will offer Bitcoin and Ethereum trading services in early 2026. Coinbase CEO: Major Banks Partnering with Coinbase on Cryptocurrency Pilot Projects Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong stated that several major banks are partnering with Coinbase on pilot projects in stablecoins, custody, and trading. He did not name specific banks. Armstrong stated, "Top banks are actively seizing this opportunity, and those institutions that resist change will ultimately be eliminated by the times." KobeissiLetter published a market analysis stating that cryptocurrency ETFs are making a comeback, with cryptocurrency funds recording $1.1 billion in inflows last week, a seven-week high. This marks a reversal of the previous four-week streak of $4.7 billion in net outflows. US cryptocurrency ETFs led the way with $994 million in inflows, followed by Canada ($98 million) and Switzerland ($24 million), while Germany saw an outflow of $57 million. Bitcoin led the inflows with a net inflow of $461 million, followed by ETH with a net inflow of $308 million. Meanwhile, investors withdrew $1.9 billion from shorting Bitcoin in ETPs. The upward momentum of cryptocurrencies is returning. BlackRock CEO Admits Previous Opposition to Bitcoin and Cryptocurrencies Was Wrong BlackRock CEO Larry Fink admitted today at the New York Times DealBook Summit that his previous opposition to Bitcoin and cryptocurrencies was a mistake. When asked, "You called cryptocurrencies tools for money laundering and theft in 2017, but now BlackRock owns the largest Bitcoin spot ETF. What happened?" Fink responded, "I have strong opinions, but that doesn't mean I'm not wrong. Through constant self-examination and meeting with thousands of clients and government leaders every year, my thinking has evolved, and my views have changed dramatically. Now BlackRock actively embraces Bitcoin." **Texas Purchases Approximately $5 Million in BlackRock IBIT ETF** According to market news, Texas has become the first U.S. state to allocate Bitcoin exposure through a state-managed investment fund—the state purchased approximately $5 million worth of BlackRock IBIT ETF under its newly established SB 21 Bitcoin Reserve framework. **BlackRock Deposits 44,140 ETH to Coinbase Prime** According to Lookonchian monitoring, BlackRock deposited 44,140 ETH, worth $135.36 million, into Coinbase Prime. **Sonnet, a publicly listed company, establishes an $888 million cryptocurrency DAT (Digital Asset Treasury)** According to Arkham ZH, US-listed Sonnet Biotherapeutics and Hyperliquid Strategies Inc. have merged to form a digital asset treasury (DAT) centered around HYPE. Currently, $888 million has been committed, with approximately 65% ​​invested in HYPE tokens and approximately 35% in US dollars. This reserve plan was officially confirmed by shareholder vote this morning. **Matrixport: Bitcoin forms higher lows, bullish signals emerge** Matrixport released a daily chart analysis stating that Bitcoin fell by more than 6% due to the unwinding of yen carry trades triggered by new speculation about a Bank of Japan interest rate hike, as well as the liquidation of nearly $1 billion in leveraged positions. Crypto-related stocks, including MicroStrategy, fell as much as 12%, further exacerbated by weak market sentiment and approximately $3.5 billion in outflows from Bitcoin ETFs in November. Following warnings from Chinese regulators about illicit digital token activities, risk aversion intensified, and Bitcoin briefly dipped below $84,000. As the stock market stabilized and US banks allowed advisors to recommend up to 4% Bitcoin allocations for wealthy clients, Bitcoin rebounded above $90,000. Vanguard's eventual opening of Bitcoin investment access to clients further boosted market sentiment. From a technical perspective, Bitcoin has formed a "higher lows" pattern, which could be a bullish signal. YZi Labs has issued a formal notice to 10X Capital, alerting CEA Industries shareholders to its disruptive behavior. The notice outlines what YZi Labs believes are material or potential breaches of fiduciary duty, deficiencies in transparency and corporate governance by 10X and its executives (including BNC CEO David Namdar and 10X founder and BNC board member Hans Thomas). YZi Labs requires 10X to provide written confirmation by December 5, 2025, that it will comply with the BNB fund management strategy presented to PIPE investors and has not mishandled BNB assets.

▌ETHZilla Acquires 20% Fully Diluted Stake in Karus for $10 Million

Nasdaq-listed Ethereum treasury company ETHZilla Corporation announced the acquisition of a 20% fully diluted stake in Karus, an AI platform for auto finance decision-making and portfolio analysis. The counterparty paid $3 million in cash and $7 million worth of ETHZilla common stock. Following the transaction, ETHZilla may explore businesses such as tokenized auto loans.

▌Circle Minted 500 Million USDC on the Solana Chain Yesterday

According to SolanaFloor monitoring, Circle minted 500 million USDC on the Solana chain yesterday.

**Important Economic Developments** **▌US November ADP Employment Change: -32,000, Expected 10,000** **On December 3rd, US November ADP employment change showed a decrease of 32,000, compared to an expected 10,000 and a previous value of 42,000.** **▌Bessant: US President Trump Has Normalized 15% to 20% Tariffs** **According to market news:** US Treasury Secretary Bessant stated that US President Trump has normalized 15% to 20% tariffs. Trade Representative Greer has done an excellent job in eliminating non-tariff barriers. Trump's view on tariffs is correct; he has a higher risk tolerance.

▌Treasury Secretary Bessant May Concurrently Serve Hassett's Current Position

According to sources, Trump's aides and allies are discussing a personnel arrangement: if Trump appoints Hassett as the next Federal Reserve Chairman, current Treasury Secretary Bessant may also serve as Director of the White House National Economic Council. This move would allow Bessant to oversee the Trump administration's economic policies comprehensively, integrating the Treasury Department's and the White House's oversight authority over economic affairs. The arrangement has not yet been finalized. A White House official stated that any changes in personnel are speculative until the president officially announces them. A Treasury Department representative did not respond to requests for comment.

▌89% Probability of a 25 Basis Point Rate Cut by the Federal Reserve in December

According to CME's "FedWatch": the probability of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates by 25 basis points in December is 89%, while the probability of keeping rates unchanged is 11%.

The probability of the Federal Reserve cutting interest rates by a cumulative 25 basis points by January next year is 64.8%, the probability of keeping rates unchanged is 7.6%, and the probability of a cumulative 50 basis point cut is 27.6%. Central bank officials from various EU countries have pointed out that dollar-backed stablecoins may pose a threat to their monetary policy-making capabilities. The stablecoin market has experienced explosive growth over the past year, largely thanks to the legal certainty in the United States. The total market capitalization of stablecoins has hit record highs every month, but European Central Bank policymakers are concerned that increased adoption of these dollar assets during crises could trigger a series of problems. Issuers of euro- and pound-backed stablecoins also acknowledge these risks, but they believe that proposed solutions such as a digital euro cannot quickly provide viable alternatives, and question whether central bank digital currencies are the right choice. Conversely, issuers believe that the key to solving the problem of Eurodollarization lies in building a thriving European stablecoin ecosystem. Monetary policymakers are already advancing the Eurozone's digital currency project. According to the European Central Bank, the project aims to reduce the Eurozone's dependence on non-European providers, help integrate the fragmented payments sector, and support innovation and competition. Significant uncertainties remain: it is unclear whether the digital euro will operate on blockchain or other proprietary systems; the proposed holding limits will offset many of the inherent advantages of private stablecoins, such as scalability and decentralized access. Whether it's a private stablecoin or a digital euro closely overseen by the European Central Bank, the sovereignty of European monetary policy depends on the development of digital currencies.
